slides.title: AI in business workflows: where the real ROI actually hides
points: Most teams automate the noisiest process first ― budget spent, P&L unchanged six months later
The real questions: which workflow first, how to build for production, how to measure ROI before you commit
This deck: high-value opportunities, common failure patterns, what production-grade looks like
Start with a structured view of opportunities: a free AI workflow audit before any build commitment

slides.title: What "AI in business workflows" actually means
points: Classic automation (RPA, rules) is fast until exceptions appear ― then it breaks
AI handles variability: NLP, ML anomaly detection, RAG for drafts ― without rigid rulebooks
Use AI where decisions matter: classification, routing, risk flagging, forecasting ― not simple field updates
That distinction tells you what to evaluate with AI vs what cheaper automation should own

slides.title: Where AI in business workflows creates real ROI
points: Best bets: high-volume decisions, measurable outcomes, meaningful cost of errors
Finance: ML on invoices often cuts manual review 60–80%; stronger controls and DPO impact
Customer operations: triage, co-pilots ― insurance case: −30% resolution time, +45% satisfaction, half the training cost
Operations: predictive maintenance and demand forecasting ― runs without a permanent data science bench

slides.title: Why most workflow AI projects fail before they ship
points: McKinsey: fewer than 30% of organisations report measurable AI value at scale ― failure is rarely the model alone
Wrong problem: annoying work automated, not consequential work
Data immaturity: fragmented, inconsistent sources undermine reliability in production
No adoption: parallel manual processes return; POC never meets integration, drift, retraining reality
Double Diamond start: most failures are prioritisation and planning ― not algorithm quality

slides.title: How to identify which workflows to automate first
points: 1) Where is time spent on intelligence-heavy, low-creativity tasks?
2) Where do manual errors cascade into cash, churn, or compliance risk?
3) Where can you measure cycle time, errors, or cost per transaction for a real business case?
Case: logistics ― exception handling (five systems, four hours each, 300/month) beat delay emails by 1,050 hours/month saved when sequenced right

slides.title: What production-grade implementation looks like
points: Production = live data, auditable outputs, monitored accuracy, retraining when distributions shift ― not a demo dataset
Integrate to ERP, CRM, HRMS, ticketing; define governance and human escalation thresholds
Dashboards the business reads; plan for drift and pipeline retraining; embed in daily tools
MLOps and engineering close the gap from stalled POC to reliable production operations

slides.title: How to model AI workflow ROI before you build
points: Baseline: time, headcount, loaded cost, monthly volume of the workflow today
Estimate assisted state with conservative cycle-time improvement (e.g. 50% documents, 30% decision-support)
Multiply savings × volume × cost; add error-reduction value if quantified
Payback under 12 months on conservative assumptions ― pursue; else revisit scope or sequencing
Gartner: strong AI ROI outcomes correlate with structured pre-build business case modelling

slides.title: The bottom line & next steps
points: Impact comes from right workflow + production standard + adoption ― not slide decks alone
Healthcare example: referral routing −91% misroutes, three days to four hours; Phase 2 funded on proof
Remember: volume, measurability, downstream consequence ― most “model failures” are scoping failures
Next step: free AI Workflow Audit ― honest priorities, realistic build path, no commitment
